analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet argues that judging China by Western democratic standards is misguided; with an 'objective & deep insight' into China’s decision-making and selection/appointment systems, one reaches a different (implied positive) conclusion. The quoted text asserts China cannot function as Western-style democracy, emphasizing efficiency, speed, and results over elections, implying the Chinese system suits its people and conditions. Evaluation by narrative: CH1 (The West is immoral, hostile and decadent): The tweet contrasts with Western democracy and criticizes 'decades of empty promises and election,' but does not depict the West as immoral/hostile or position China as a victim; it's more about system suitability. Not promoted. CH2 (China is a benevolent power): No focus on international conduct, multilateralism, or global support. Not promoted. CH3 (China has an epic history): No historical arc or past adversities mentioned. Not promoted. CH4 (China has an appropriate political system and honorable values): Yes. The tweet explicitly defends China’s political system as appropriate and more democratic in its own way when properly understood, emphasizing efficiency, results, and alignment with Chinese preferences; it rejects Western-style democracy as the benchmark. This fits advocacy of China’s domestic political model and values. CH5 (The government of the Chinese Communist Party succeeds): While it praises efficiency and results orientation, it does not specifically claim developmental successes or achievements; the emphasis is normative/structural rather than outcome-based. Weakly implied but not explicit. Not promoted. CH6 (Culture/nature/heritage): Not relevant. Conclusion: Only CH4 is clearly promoted.
